perturbation.original_text: Sponsor will be included as co-presenting partner upon on-site signage.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'as co-presenting partner upon on-site signage', the modified text creates ambiguity. The original text clearly states the Sponsor will be acknowledged as a co-presenting partner on signage. The modified version only confirms on-site inclusion, this can be in any form, which makes the original intention of the co-presenting partnership unclear. This contrasts with clause 1(a) which states that the sponsor will be a co-presenting sponsor
perturbation.contradicted_text: During the term of this Agreement, the Sponsor will be a co-presenting sponsor of FLOODfest Chicago at Virgin Hotel, which entitles the Sponsor to the following:
perturbation.justification: The original text specified the sponsor would be included 'as co-presenting partner upon on-site signage.' The changed text removes 'as co-presenting partner upon on-site signage,' only stating the sponsor will be included 'on-site.' This weakens the original agreement regarding the level and form of on-site inclusion. The removal creates ambiguity which contradicts the clause stating 'the Sponsor will be a co-presenting sponsor of FLOODfest Chicago' because the sponsor is no longer guaranteed to be acknowledged as a co-presenting partner on the signage itself.

perturbation.original_text: If the Sponsor fails to pay ANTHEMIC within the designated time period, then interest will begin to accrue immediately on the past due amount at the rate of the lesser of the maximum amount allowed by law or 10% annually.
perturbation.changed_text: If the Sponsor fails to pay ANTHEMIC within the designated time period, then interest will begin to accrue immediately on the past due amount.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specified that interest accrues at the rate of 'the lesser of the maximum amount allowed by law or 10% annually.' By removing this specification, the contract no longer defines how the interest rate will be determined. This omission leads to uncertainty, especially if the 'maximum amount allowed by law' is vague. This creates a contradiction because section 2(a) provides dates where payment must be made, but the exact rate is now uncertain.
perturbation.contradicted_text: To be a sponsor of the Event, the Sponsor will pay ANTHEMIC the following: Fee Due Dates $75,000 - Sponsorship Fee $50,000 due upon receipt of this term sheet $25,000 final balance payment due upon announcement date of July 15, 2015.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined the interest rate that would accrue if the Sponsor failed to pay on time, as the 'lesser of the maximum amount allowed by law or 10% annually.' The changed text removes this definition, leaving the interest rate undefined. Section 2(a), 'To be a sponsor of the Event, the Sponsor will pay ANTHEMIC the following: Fee Due Dates $75,000 - Sponsorship Fee $50,000 due upon receipt of this term sheet $25,000 final balance payment due upon announcement date of July 15, 2015,' specifies payment due dates. Without a defined interest rate, it's unclear how much the Sponsor owes if they pay late, directly contradicting the certainty implied by the payment schedule in Section 2(a). This omission creates a significant ambiguity and potential dispute about the final balance due, fitting the 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: Before April 1, 2016 ANTHEMIC will not discuss in any manner with any person or entity (other than the Sponsor) to be a sponsor of the Event in the Video App category. If ANTHEMIC produces the Event in 2016 and seeks a sponsor in the category, ANTHEMIC will first contact the Sponsor and provide the Sponsor with written notice (the "Notice) of the terms under which the Sponsor can be the category sponsor for the 2016 Event. The Sponsor will have 15 days from receipt of the Notice to accept the terms to be the category sponsor of the 2014 Event. If the Sponsor decides not to be the category sponsor of the 2016 Event or fails to timely respond to the Notice, then ANTHEMIC may approach other parties to be the category sponsor.
perturbation.changed_text: Before April 1, 2016 ANTHEMIC will not discuss in any manner with any person or entity (other than the Sponsor) to be a sponsor of the Event in the Video App category. If ANTHEMIC produces the Event in 2016 and seeks a sponsor in the category, ANTHEMIC will first contact the Sponsor and provide the Sponsor with written notice. The Sponsor will have 15 days from receipt of the Notice to accept the terms to be the category sponsor of the 2014 Event. If the Sponsor decides not to be the category sponsor of the 2016 Event or fails to timely respond to the Notice, then ANTHEMIC may approach other parties to be the category sponsor.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the section 'the terms under which the Sponsor can be the category sponsor for the 2016 Event'. This creates an in-text contradiction, by reffering to the sponsor being the category sponsor for the event, without specifying the terms which such thing will occur. This introduces ambiguity and potential for disputes, this contradicts with the section that talks about the terms and agreements.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Before April 1, 2016 ANTHEMIC will not discuss in any manner with any person or entity (other than the Sponsor) to be a sponsor of the Event in the Video App category. If ANTHEMIC produces the Event in 2016 and seeks a sponsor in the category, ANTHEMIC will first contact the Sponsor and provide the Sponsor with written notice. The Sponsor will have 15 days from receipt of the Notice to accept the terms to be the category sponsor of the 2014 Event. If the Sponsor decides not to be the category sponsor of the 2016 Event or fails to timely respond to the Notice, then ANTHEMIC may approach other parties to be the category sponsor.
perturbation.justification: The omission of 'the terms under which the Sponsor can be the category sponsor for the 2016 Event' in the changed text directly contradicts the implied requirement for such terms to exist and be communicated. The original text ensures that the Sponsor receives a notice outlining the terms of sponsorship. The changed text, by removing this obligation, introduces ambiguity and potential disputes, thus contradicting the section's intended purpose of a clear agreement on sponsorship terms, and makes the agreement unenforceable.
